excerpt: The Messenger of God declared, 'Verily the sun shall rise from the west after three thousand years'—a statement referring to the Mahdi, who emerged from the western regions.
source_location_hint: Esoteric interpretation of prophetic utterances on the appearance of the Mahdi

excerpt: The Prophet pronounced the exoteric utterance and bound his community to it, so that they might progress toward the esoteric sense with sincerity. After externally observing the letter, sincerity becomes obligatory. Through the exoteric utterance, one enters the domain of Islam and the sword is withheld; yet he does not enter paradise.
source_location_hint: On the distinction between exoteric obedience and esoteric faith

excerpt: 'There is no power and no strength save in Allah'—this proclaims that the First Intellect alone possesses power. All potency emanates from Him and returns to Him.
source_location_hint: On the perfect unity and power of the First Intellect

excerpt: All of the major stations attain one hundred forms. When the count reaches one hundred, it is time for the elevation of rank. The Final Emanator brings forth instances numbering one hundred and ten, signifying transcendence of ten levels of proof-bearing and entry into veiledness.
source_location_hint: On the perfection of spiritual stations and their culmination

excerpt: Allah has apportioned faith among all the organs of the human body such that the faith of each organ is distinct from that of another.
source_location_hint: On the distribution of faith throughout the human body and organs

excerpt: The effect of haste and delay is established in the organs of the human body. When an invitation is presented in the world of intentions, if a person denies a limit, then upon birth he witnesses the effect of his denial as a defect in his soul. If he confesses the limit and repents, in the next sphere the defect vanishes; otherwise, the effect of one sphere continues to impress upon the next.
source_work: Kitāb al-ʿĀlim wa-l-Ghulām (The Master and the Youth)
source_location_hint: On causality across spheres and the rectification of defects through repentance

excerpt: Revelation began with the unfolding of the first cycle. His true son and successor shall be the next unfolding cycle. Yet in part, he established his son as Imam and made him the regent of the Eighth Intellect, elevating the Eighth Intellect's rank. The Ninth then merged into the Eighth, and thus the Third Intellect and its sphere merged into the Holy Presence.
source_location_hint: On the cyclical manifestation of Imamate and the merging of intellects

excerpt: Thus there are six thrones of the Qāʾim, and beneath each lie the thrones of the Imams of seven thousand years. When the seventh Qāʾim arrives, he gathers all his Imams of seven thousand years and collects the previous six thrones, merging them into a single great throne. In this manner, there are seven unveilings and seventy in a single epoch.
source_location_hint: On the hierarchical structure of Qāʾims and Imams across cosmic cycles

excerpt: The Lote-tree of the Limit is the essence of the First Intellect, and the Garden of Abode is the station of the Garden of Abode. When the Messenger of Allah ascended in Miʿrāj, he beheld Allah through a great eye. The Lote-tree of the Limit is Mawlānā ʿĀmir (ʿAbd al-Muttalib), and the Garden of Abode is Mawlānā ʿImrān (Abū Ṭālib).
source_work: Asrār al-Nutaqāʾ (The Secrets of the Speakers)
source_location_hint: On the identification of the Lote-tree and Garden with the great ancestors of the Prophet

excerpt: The Eighth Intellect became convinced that the souls under his charge had become so oppressed and their density so entrenched that it could not be lifted by keeping them in the spiritual world. He resolved to create a world in which the disobedient company could be brought down through the human condition and invited anew.
source_work: Raḥat al-ʿAql (The Rest of the Intellect)
source_location_hint: On the descent of the Eighth Intellect and the creation of the physical realm for spiritual rectification

excerpt: Just as the invitation of the seven intellects was established in the spiritual world by the command of the First Intellect, the Eighth Intellect likewise resolved to establish his invitation in the physical world. The difference lay in this: the invitation of the First Intellect transcends time and space, whereas the invitation of the Eighth Intellect operates within the confines of temporal succession.
source_work: Kashf al-Maḥjūb (The Unveiling of the Concealed)
source_location_hint: On the parallel structures of divine invitation across spiritual and physical realms
